goal: Fix incorrect result counts, stale expanded state, and data correctness issues across all report types so users can trust the numbers
not_building: Avg cost decimal precision (needs decision on rule)
Net Worth graph styling (fonts, axes, date density)
Payee column when grouping by Payee

steps.intent: Fix result count display to match query type. FilterChipRow currently always sources counts from TransactionFilter.apply(), showing transaction counts even for position and snapshot queries. Add a QueryResult.counts selector that branches on query type (via .match()) to return { filtered, total, itemLabel }. QueryResultPage reads this selector and passes explicit counts to FilterChipRow (which already accepts filteredCount/totalCount props). This also fixes the position-count-for-non-investment-account bug — when the query returns 0 positions, the count should show 0.
steps.scope: selectors.js (new QueryResult.counts memoized selector branching on query type), QueryResultPage.jsx (read selector, pass counts to FilterChipRow). FilterChipRow.jsx is NOT edited — it already accepts explicit count props. Test: unit test for QueryResult.counts selector, integration assertion that filtering to non-investment account shows 0 positions.
key_constraints: Count source must branch on query type: TransactionQuery uses transaction count, PositionQuery uses position count, SnapshotQuery uses snapshot row count
FilterChipRow must still work with implicit counts (from selector) when explicit props are not passed — registers still use the old path
The 'filtered from N' denominator must also reflect the query-type-appropriate total, not the transaction total
steps.acceptance: Position report shows '32 positions (filtered from 32)' or no filtered-from suffix; filtering to a non-investment account shows '0 positions'; snapshot report shows row count, not transaction count.
steps.notes: Added _queryResultCounts selector branching via fallbackIR.match(). TransactionQuery delegates to existing _filterCounts, PositionQuery counts leaf nodes via _countPositionLeaves recursive helper, SnapshotQuery counts snapshots.length or nodes.length, AccountQuery counts result.length. Memoized with same ENGINE_STATE_KEYS. QueryResultPage reads counts selector and passes filteredCount/totalCount to FilterChipRow. FilterChipRow already accepted optional count props — no changes needed. All 230 integration tests pass.

steps.intent: Reset expanded row state when groupBy changes, fixing both the stale-expansion bug and the disclosure triangle visual mismatch. Handle in the root reducer: when SetTransactionFilter changes the groupBy field, also clear treeExpansion to {} in the same atomic state transition. This follows the existing precedent where ResetTransactionFilters chains cross-slice updates.
steps.scope: store/reducer.js (detect groupBy change in SetTransactionFilter handler, clear treeExpansion atomically). GroupByFilterChip is NOT edited — coordination belongs in the reducer, not the UI component. Test: integration assertion in category-report or positions-report that changing groupBy resets expanded rows.
key_constraints: Only reset treeExpansion when groupBy actually changes — not on every SetTransactionFilter
Expanded state lives in ViewUiState Redux slice (per docs/solutions), not module-level state
Disclosure triangle rendering uses row.getIsExpanded() from TanStack Table controlled state — verify the controlled-state loop (Redux → prop → TanStack → render) works after reset
steps.acceptance: Expanding rows in one groupBy, then switching groupBy, shows all rows collapsed with triangles pointing right (▶).
steps.notes: Added setTransactionFilterWithGroupByReset in root reducer. Detects when action.changes.groupBy differs from existing groupBy and atomically clears treeExpansion to {} on the viewUiState slice. Follows ResetTransactionFilters precedent for cross-slice updates. All 73 integration tests pass (category-report + positions-report).

steps.intent: Investigate and fix Chase Sapphire showing positive balance in Net Worth Over Time. The snapshot engine sums transaction amounts directly for cash accounts (including credit cards). Hypothesis: QIF import records credit card purchases as positive amounts (money spent) rather than negative, so summing gives a positive balance instead of the expected negative liability. Investigate the actual transaction data for the Chase Sapphire account, check QIF sign conventions for credit cards, and fix the balance computation or transaction enrichment to produce correct net worth values for liabilities.
steps.scope: query-language/src/run-financial-query.js (collectBalanceSnapshot), query-language/src/types/enriched-account.js (balance computation), possibly qif-parser if sign convention is wrong at import. Test: unit test asserting credit card balance is negative in snapshot result.
key_constraints: Credit cards and Other Liability accounts should contribute negative values to net worth
Fix must not break bank account (positive asset) or investment account balance computation
If the fix is in the query engine, it affects all snapshot reports — verify no regressions
steps.acceptance: Chase Sapphire shows negative balance (liability) in Net Worth Over Time snapshot; bank accounts remain positive.
steps.notes: Root cause: collectBalanceSnapshot passed empty investmentAccountIdsInScope to computePositions when account filter excluded investment accounts. computePositions treats empty selectedAccountIds as 'all accounts', so ALL investment positions were included in the total even when filtering to a credit card. Fix: early-return with cashTotal only when investmentAccountIdsInScope is empty. Unit test added to run-financial-query.tap.js confirming credit card filtered balance is negative (-75). All 157+51 tests pass.

steps.intent: Investigate uncategorized row appearing in 'trans' filter results for Category by Year. Research confirms: regex /trans/i matches 'Uncategorized' because it contains the substring 'trans'. Determine whether this is WAI or needs a fix (e.g., match only category display names, not synthetic labels). Also investigate the 'Food % of Income' row with all dashes — is it a computed/derived row that persists after filtering?
steps.scope: query-language/src/apply-chip-filters.js (filter field list), query-language/src/category-tree.js (synthetic row labels, computed rows). Investigation may reveal this is WAI — if so, document finding in notes and move on.
key_constraints: The text filter searches 8 transaction fields (payee, category, memo, amount, date, number, investmentAction, account) — this is intentional broad matching
If 'Uncategorized' matching 'trans' is deemed WAI, no code change needed — just document the finding
steps.acceptance: Root cause identified and either fixed or documented as WAI with Jeff's approval.
steps.notes: Both issues are WAI. (1) Uncategorized matching 'trans': 65 transfer transactions (payees like 'Transfer to Savings', memos like 'Cash transfer in') match /trans/i on payee/memo fields. They're uncategorized because QIF transfers use account references instead of categories. Filter and grouping both correct — the unintuitive part is that the user can't see WHY the row matched. Added to brainstorm Deferred list as a UI design question. (2) 'Food % of Income' with dashes: computed row divides Food by Income. When Income rows are filtered out, denominator is 0/undefined → dashes. WAI — no Income means the ratio is undefined.

steps.intent: Format snapshot date column headers from raw ISO (2025-04-30) to friendly format (Apr 2025). The toTreeValueColumn factory in QueryResultPage uses the ISO date string directly as the column header. Add a local helper in QueryResultPage that formats ISO dates based on the query interval (monthly → 'Apr 2025', quarterly → 'Q1 2025', yearly → '2025'). The interval must be threaded from the baseQueryIR metadata to the column factories.
steps.scope: QueryResultPage.jsx (toTreeValueColumn/toTreePivotColumns — format header, thread interval from baseQueryIR or result metadata). Local helper only — no shared utility unless a second call site emerges.
key_constraints: Format should vary by interval: monthly → 'Apr 2025', quarterly → 'Q1 2025', yearly → '2025'
Column data keys remain ISO strings — only the display header changes
steps.acceptance: Net Worth Over Time column headers show 'Apr 2025' instead of '2025-04-30'; other interval formats render appropriately.
steps.notes: Added F.toColumnHeader local helper in QueryResultPage that detects ISO date columns via regex and formats based on interval: monthly → 'Apr 2025', quarterly → 'Q1 2025', yearly → '2025'. Non-ISO columns (like year strings from categoryByYear) pass through unchanged. Threaded baseQueryIR.interval through toTreePivotColumns → toTreeValueColumn. Updated 4 integration test assertions from ISO dates to formatted headers. All 281 tests pass.

verification: Investment Positions report shows position count, not transaction count
Filtering Investment Positions to Chase Sapphire (non-investment) shows '0 positions' with empty table
Net Worth Over Time shows snapshot row count, not transaction count
Spending by Category: expand rows, change groupBy → all rows collapsed, triangles show ▶
Chase Sapphire shows negative balance in Net Worth Over Time
Net Worth Over Time column headers show friendly date format (Apr 2025)
Category by Year filtered by 'trans': verify Uncategorized behavior matches decision
All existing integration tests pass (engine-reports, category-report, positions-report)
